member.name: M:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.AuthController.Login(Shared.DataTransferObjects.Auth.LoginRequestDto)
member.summary: Login and receive a JWT token.

member.name: M:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.CartController.AddItem(Shared.DataTransferObjects.Cart.AddToCartRequestDto)
member.summary: Add a product to the cart.

member.name: M:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.CartController.UpdateItem(System.Guid,Shared.DataTransferObjects.Cart.UpdateCartItemRequestDto)
member.summary: Update the quantity of a cart item. Set quantity to 0 to remove.

member.name: M:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.CartController.RemoveItem(System.Guid)
member.summary: Remove a specific item from the cart.

member.name: M:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.OrdersController.Checkout(Shared.DataTransferObjects.Order.CheckoutRequestDto)
member.summary: Checkout: converts the current cart into an order and creates a payment authorization URL. Returns the provider's hosted payment page URL.

member.name: M:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.OrdersController.VerifyPayment(System.Guid)
member.summary: Verify payment status for an order by actively querying the payment provider. Called by the frontend immediately after the user returns from the payment page so the order status is updated without waiting for a webhook.

member.name: M:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.OrdersController.GetOrders
member.summary: Get all orders for the current user.

member.name: M:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.OrdersController.GetOrder(System.Guid)
member.summary: Get a specific order by ID.

member.name: M:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.ProductsController.GetProducts(System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Get all active products with optional search and category filter.

member.name: M:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.ProductsController.GetProduct(System.Guid)
member.summary: Get a single product by ID.

member.name: M:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.ProductsController.CreateProduct(Shared.DataTransferObjects.Product.CreateProductRequestDto)
member.summary: Create a new product. Admin only.

member.name: M:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.ProductsController.UpdateProduct(System.Guid,Shared.DataTransferObjects.Product.UpdateProductRequestDto)
member.summary: Update an existing product. Admin only.

member.name: T:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.WebhookController
member.summary:
Handles incoming webhooks from payment providers (Paystack and Flutterwave).
These endpoints MUST NOT inherit [Authorize] from a parent controller — they are called by external servers that have no JWT token. Putting them in their own controller (not nested under OrdersController which is [Authorize]) ensures [AllowAnonymous] is not shadowed by a class-level [Authorize] attribute.
The webhook 401 bug was caused by the endpoints living inside the [Authorize] OrdersController. ASP.NET Core's JWT middleware challenges the request before the [AllowAnonymous] attribute is evaluated in some pipeline configurations. Moving them here eliminates that ambiguity entirely.

member.name: M:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.WebhookController.FlutterwaveWebhook
member.summary: Flutterwave webhook endpoint. Called by Flutterwave after payment events. Authenticated via the "verif-hash" header (plain secret string comparison).

member.name: M:ECommerce.Presentation.Controllers.WebhookController.PaystackWebhook
member.summary: Paystack webhook endpoint. Called by Paystack after payment events. Authenticated via HMAC-SHA512 in the "x-paystack-signature" header. Paystack requires a 200 response within 5 seconds or it retries.
